_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in interpreting compiled and interpretive programming languages.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in writing production-quality Python code for data processing, feature engineering, model training, and evaluation, including documentation and unit testing.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.464), TF-S-001 (TF, cosine: 0.464).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.1/10 (raw 0.315). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. DCWF-only terms [compiled, interpreting, interpretive, languages, programming] vs WIDAI-only terms [code, data, documentation, engineering, evaluation, feature] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.871 (P=0.885, R=0.858). P exceeds R by 0.027 — the DCWF element's 8 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.9%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in interpreting compiled and interpretive programming languages." and "Skill in writing production-quality Python code for data processing, feature eng". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.483 > STS 0.315 (gap +0.168). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.0%. Shared tokens: [skill]. DCWF-only: [compiled, interpreting, interpretive, languages, programming]. WIDAI-only: [code, data, documentation, engineering, evaluation, feature].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.9% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.168 with low STS 0.315. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
